pattern: A sliding window of fixed width combined with a seen-set catches every 10-letter substring that appears more than once in O(n) time and space.
hints: Every 10-letter substring is a potential candidate; you need to know which ones you have encountered before.
Keep two sets: one for substrings seen at least once, another for substrings already confirmed as duplicates so they are only added to the result once.
Slide a window of width 10 across the string; on each step check membership in the first set before adding to the result set.

edge_inputs.description: string shorter than 10 characters, no possible 10-mer
all same character, length 11 gives exactly one repeated 10-mer
a repeated sequence appearing three times must still appear in result exactly once

stress.description: long DNA string with planted repeated 10-mer at random positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    bases = 'ACGT'
    n = max(20, size)
    chars = [bases[rng.randrange(4)] for _ in range(n)]
    motif = ''.join(bases[rng.randrange(4)] for _ in range(10))
    pos1 = rng.randrange(0, n - 20)
    pos2 = rng.randrange(pos1 + 10, n - 10)
    for i, c in enumerate(motif):
        chars[pos1 + i] = c
        chars[pos2 + i] = c
    return [''.join(chars)]
